videos.title: Dhaka City | Capital of Bangladesh | आईये घुमें ढाका शहर 🌿🇧🇩
videos.description:
Dhaka City | Capita of Bangladesh | emerging city of South Asia

About Dhaka city :

Dhaka, the capital city of Bangladesh, is a vibrant and bustling metropolis with a rich history and a vibrant culture. With its blend of old-world charm and modern developments, Dhaka offers visitors a unique and exciting experience. From its bustling markets and historic landmarks to its delicious cuisine and warm hospitality, there is something for everyone in this dynamic city. 


---- Places to visit in Dhaka city ----

(1) Bangladesh National Museum

(2) Dhakeshwari Temple

(3) Central Shaheed Minar

(4) Dhaka railway station

(5) Lalbagh Fort, Dhaka

(6) Tara Masjid

(7) Dhaka New Market

(8) Dhaka city tour

(9) Bangladesh National Zoo

(10) Baitul Mukarram Mosque

videos.title: Exploring The World's Most Crowded City! (Featuring @RafsanTheChotobhai)
videos.description:
We explore the beautiful city of Dhaka and the culture of Ramadan in beautiful Bangladesh.  After that we stayed longer to learn more about the people of Bangladesh and their hopes for the future inshAllah! 🇧🇩

We are so sorry this video is late, we had some footage and editing issues but we were finally able to put this video together, we hope you all learn more about this incredible country, and we hope it makes the people of Bangladesh proud inshAllah! 

Much love and God bless ❤️✌️

CLICK HERE TO SUBSCRIBE FOR MORE VIDEOS: https://www.youtube.com/khalidalameri?sub_confirmation=1

FOLLOW US HERE:
Our Tik Tok: https://vm.tiktok.com/HgGfPA
Salama's Snapchat: https://www.snapchat.com/add/salamaskin
Salama's Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/salamamohamed
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/khalidalameri
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/khalidalameri

Music by Epidemic Sound: https://www.epidemicsound.com/campaign/invite-a-creator/?_us=Referral&_usx=60zjus&utm_source=ref_program&utm_medium=affiliate&utm_campaign=60zjus
tags: most crowded cities in the world
videos.transcript: Being one of the most densely populated countries in the world, the image of Bangladesh that has been shown by the media is one of a country facing a lot of difficulties. But today, I wanna show you a different image. So the team and I flew four hours to Dhaka, the capital city of Bangladesh during the holy month of Ramadan. Touchdown Bangladesh. We're here. This is when the country is the most alive. Where we can show you the rich culture, the incredible food, and above all, the heartwarming kindness of the people of this country. Today, you will see Bangladesh. - Welcome to Bangladesh. - Welcome to Bangladesh. - Welcome to Bangladesh. - Welcome to Bangladesh. - Welcome to Bangladesh. - Welcome to Bangladesh. And what better way to explore a country, than with our local friend and fellow YouTuber, Rafsan TheChotoBhai. - You're really good. - Thank you, man. - Yeah. What are we gonna do today? - Whatever you want to do today. - We're gonna go to Chowk Bazaar, then! - [Khalid] Alright. I'm ready. - I'm gonna test you, but you gotta blend in. You gotta turn Bangladeshi. - Bro, I will be so Bangladeshi that you will question how Bangladeshi you are. - Whoa. Okay. I'll act like I'm from Dubai. (both laughing) Since we are here during Ramadan, we are headed to the legendary Chowk Bazaar, one of Dhaka's oldest markets, to buy some items for Iftar later that evening. We are at Chowk Bazaar, here in Dhaka, Bangladesh, just before Iftar It is intense. It is beautiful. There are a lot of people coming together. You know, we're reaching towards the end of Ramadan. Everyone's out on the streets. Everyone's trying to get their food, sell their food, get everything done, in like a matter of a couple of hours. Just to put things into context for you, this is not a long street. I can literally stand on one side and I see the end of the street there, but it's just so much is packed in here, in terms of the stalls, and that's why all the people come down. This is the energy I live for. (Vendor yells in foreign language) (Repeats foreign language) - So the marketing strategy here is to announce louder than the other person who's down there. And it seems to be working 'cause there's a big crowd gather around now. - (yells in foreign language) - (yells in foreign language) - (yells in foreign language) The first thing that we have been asked to try is a Bangladeshi Iftar delicacy that is called Beguni. It is eggplant dipped in chickpea flour batter then deep fried and then take it home and you enjoy. In India, it's called Jalebi in Bangladesh is called Jilapi, which is pretty much the same thing. The flour batter, deep fried, sugar syrup, all that goodness exactly the same in Bangladesh as in India. - And obviously whether you walk at the beginning of the street or the end of the street or the middle of the street, one thing you're gonna see everywhere are dates. Dates are critical in Ramadan It's the way that we break our fast, so we're gonna get a bunch of these too. All right, everyone. So, we have just finished up at Chowk Bazaar. As you can see, this is the result. The love and the energy of the people all coming together was just like one of the best feelings in the world for me. And I just can't wait to carry this energy to Iftar at the mosque. So, we're gonna go pray. Then, we're gonna head to the mosque for the (speaks foreign language) See you there. All right. Now, we're at Baitul Mukarram National Mosque, the biggest mosque in Bangladesh right here in Dhaka. To enjoy our Iftar InshAllah And it is packed. There are people from everywhere mashAllah There is food distributions happening. There are people here to say their prayers, to have Iftar, and families coming together to enjoy Iftar and the break of fast together. Absolutely beautiful. Now, we're breaking our fast. We've got the food that we got at the Chowk Bazaar now. Bismillah All right, they have just called the prayer. So, now we're about to head to prayer Insha'Allah right after we've broken our fast. Being able to pray amongst thousands of fellow Muslims in some of the biggest mosques around the world is always special to me. Even though we come from very different backgrounds and don't speak the same language, we share the same moment. All in harmony. The next day, we wanted to get a more intimate look into what Ramadan is like in Bangladesh. More specifically, in a Dhaka household. Okay, day two in Bangladesh. We've already been to the Iftar market. We have been for Iftar at the mosque, but now we're gonna go visit Rafsan TheChotoBhai's house for Iftar with his family. Salam Alaikum, how are you? - We have these for you. - Oh, thank you. - Thank you so much for having us. There are a few things mashAllah We also heard that you like Mithai, so we got you some Mithai. - Thank you very much. - She loves sweets. Me too. I have a sweet tooth. Yeah. So, lots of sweets tonight. - Too. (both laughing) The first step to any home visit during Ramadan is here in the kitchen. This is the center of the household. And this is where we begin. So Rafsan's mom is officially a Raduni. And what Raduni is someone who is an expert in the art of cooking. And here we are having the honor to see it all being set up. This is the Nargisi kebab. It is a egg wrapped potato. No, potato wrapped egg. And then we're gonna do what we always do to great food, deep fry it. (upbeat music plays) So, what's your favorite thing about Ramadan? - She likes praying, making meals and preparing Iftar, and then having that spiritual connection with Allah. You're fasting all day. You're really hungry and you're doing it because you want to develop a spiritual connection. You're coming back home. You're having Iftar with your family. It's a lovely thing. And then I think there's another aspect where you're giving back to the people. Right? Right. Because my mom gives back to the people a lot. So, right now we are gonna be making Beef Tehari. It's like marinated for a long time. You can understand she started at least five to six hours ago. - Oh wow. And now it is getting cooked in ghee. And then what happens is once this rice is ready, we add the beef to it. The flip. Now, we have the Beef Tehari. Wow. - This is the Nargisi Kebab. - Smoked verki. - And we got the Chhena. - And of course, the dates! - Jalebi. - Beguni. - Piyaji. - Salad. (all laughing) - So now what happens right before Iftar, is they all sit in the living room, watch the TV, and wait for the Adhan to come on the TV. Finally, it was time to try all the delicious dishes that we helped prepare. And of course, break our fast with a date. After a long day of fasting, everyone had come together and having received such a warm welcome into Rafsan's family, we felt at home and the food of course was phenomenal. So we've just finished up Iftar at Rafsan's home with his beautiful family MashaAllah Auntie cooking us all this incredible food right here from Bangladesh. And you really feel that Bangladeshi love. That hospitality that comes together. And just to experience Ramadan in a home here, you really get the true essence of the love and the hospitality of the country mashAllah As we were finishing our meal and thanking Rafsan's family, I decided we needed to stay in Bangladesh a little longer. I felt we hadn't seen enough. We came here to document Ramadan, but little by little I realized that there was so much more that I wanted to learn and how much more I wanted to talk to the people who live here. (upbeat music) The next day, Rafsan took us to the more modern neighborhoods of Dhaka to show us parts of the city that aren't usually featured when describing Bangladesh. We are now in Gulshan in Dhaka and it's one of the busiest streets in the city. It is a signal of the modernization of Dhaka. You're seeing a lot of high rise buildings come up. A lot of restaurants, a lot of commerce, a lot of people starting businesses here. So fun fact about Jamuna Future Park, the shopping mall here in Bangladesh. It's actually the biggest mall in south Asia and the 24th biggest mall in the world. Not enough of this part of Bangladesh is shown. (music intensifies) We also organized a meet and greet to connect more with the people of Bangladesh. To learn more about their country and how they feel about its future. Bangladesh is typically portrayed as being overpopulated. And you can definitely feel that at times. But what I felt when I spoke to the youth was an overwhelming sense of energy and hospitality. For me, what I've noticed here is the energy of the people like the youth here, is incredible. The media likes to focus on the negative. On our differences, but from my experience, the reality doesn't align with this image. - In all the world, people think so many different things about us, which are mostly negative. We are misjudged in so many different ways. - We always see what's negative. And that is what we kind of think about. You know what? I don't want to do this. I don't want to go here. - The mainstream media has not been kind when it comes to the image of this country. And that's not fair at all. - When you actually come here, everyone's proven wrong. It's the other way around. Through the social media, there's a lot of stuff that is not going out. So I believe besides food, Bangladesh needs to be shown to the world in many aspects. - People think about Bangladesh very densely populated here. I would like to say everyone come Bangladesh and explore our hospitality. - Mm. So a lot of people, but a lot of nice people. - Yes. - I think you can feel the warmth already. The people that there are very, very friendly. They will come eat with you and they love you. - [Khalid] But like every country, Bangladesh faces some difficulties. But the youth we met were optimistic and excited about the future of their country. - Bangladeshi is progressing day by day. So, this is a very good thing. - I'm a citizen of Bangladesh. I am really excited for our future. I want to see my country as a better country. I want to see my country as a developed country. - When you come to Bangladesh, maybe after three years, you can see a completely changed and new Bangladesh. So, it's economically growing. So really hopeful for the future. Visiting Bangladesh, has been one of the most eye opening experiences of my travels. To me, the people of Bangladesh are proof that despite the stories that are being told about you, it's not what people say about you, but it's what you do every day that creates your reality. Maybe that is why they're one of the fastest growing economies in the world right now. So I encourage everyone, when you are told something about a country, especially something negative, before you believe it, visit and learn for yourself. You have no idea how surprised you'll be. Until next time, Bangladesh. Much love and God bless. (soft music)

safetyNotes: Even though we come from very different backgrounds and don't speak the same language, we share the same moment
I don't want to do this
I don't want to go here
recommendations: The love and the energy of the people all coming together was just like one of the best feelings in the world for me

videos.title: Dhaka City | Capital of New Bangladesh | Dhaka | Modern Bangladesh 🇧🇩
videos.description:
Dhaka City | Capital of New Bangladesh | Dhaka | Modern Bangladesh

Welcome to my complete Dhaka City Tour, where we explore the vibrant capital of Bangladesh like never before! From its rich history to modern skyscrapers, from iconic famous places to mouth-watering street food, this video showcases the true spirit of Dhaka & New Bangladesh.

What you’ll see in this video:
🏙️ A walk through modern & traditional Dhaka city
🍢 Delicious and authentic Dhaka street food
📍 Historical sites and famous landmarks of Dhaka
📖 Interesting facts about Dhaka’s history and culture
🌆 The emerging face of New Bangladesh

Whether you're planning to visit Dhaka, curious about Bangladesh’s growth, or simply love city vlogs — this video is for you!

👉 Don’t forget to LIKE, COMMENT, and SUBSCRIBE for more real and raw travel content.
📢 Share this video with your friends who’d love to explore Dhaka!


_____________________________________________________________

videos.title: ঢাকার শীর্ষ ১০টি বিখ্যাত দর্শনীয় স্থান | Amazing Top 10 Beautiful Places to Visit Dhaka Bangladesh
videos.description:
ঢাকার শীর্ষ ১০টি বিখ্যাত দর্শনীয় স্থান | Amazing Top 10 Beautiful Place Of Dhaka Bangladesh
ছুটির দিনে বাসায় বসে অবসর সময় কাটাচ্ছেন? অন্যান্য ব্যাস্ত দিনগুলোর একঘেয়েমি দূর করতে পরিবার নিয়ে কোথাও ঘুরে আসাটা কিন্তু মন্দ নয়। তাই আপনি যদি ঢাকাবাসি হন কিংবা ঢাকার আসেপাশে থাকেন তাহলে ছুটির দিনে ঢাকার ভিতর অবস্থানকৃত ১০টি স্থানে পরিবার নিয়ে ঘুরে আসতে পারেন। তো চলুন দেখে নেওয়া যাক

ছুটির দিনে ঢাকার শীর্ষ ১০টি বিখ্যাত দর্শনীয় স্থান ছাড়াও ঢাকা সিটির ভিতর অবস্থানকৃত আরো কিছু স্থান যেখানে ঘুরে আসতে পারেন। 

বঙ্গবন্ধু সামরিক জাদুঘর (বিজয় সরণী), সোহরাওয়ার্দী উদ্যান, কেন্দ্রীয় শহীদ মিনার, ঢাকা বিশ্ববিদ্যালয়ের টিএসসি, দোয়েল চত্তর, শিল্পকলা একাডেমী, ঢাকা বিশ্ববিদ্যালয়ের মধুর ক্যান্টিন, বঙ্গবন্ধু নভোথিয়েটার,বাংলাদেশ সামরিক জাদুঘর, রায়ের বাজার বধ্যভূমি, বসুন্ধরা সিটি কমপ্লেক্স, যমুনা ফিউচার পার্ক, শিশুমেলা, পুরান ঢাকা, বুড়িগঙ্গা নদী, ধানমন্ডি লেক, ম্যাটাডোর পার্ক,ইত্যাদি সহ ঢাকার একটু দূরে উত্তরা দিয়াবাড়ি, ৩০০ ফিট এর মত স্থানেও ঘুরে আসতে পারেন।

videos.title: ঢাকার ভেতরে উইকেন্ডে ঘুরে আসার সেরা ৫টি জায়গা | Top 5 Tourist Places in Dhaka ||
videos.description:
#DhakaTravelVlog #ঢাকারভেতরে_ঘুরার_জায়গা #dhaka 

ঢাকা শহর মানেই শুধু যানজট আর ব্যস্ততা নয়!
আজকের ভিডিওতে আমি আপনাদের দেখাবো ঢাকার ভেতরে ঘুরে দেখার সেরা ৬টি জায়গা, যেখানে আপনি পাবেন শান্তি, ইতিহাস, অ্যাডভেঞ্চার আর দারুণ খাবারের এক অনন্য অভিজ্ঞতা।
ছুটির দিনে পরিবার, বন্ধু বা প্রিয়জনকে নিয়ে ঢাকার বাইরে না গিয়েও ঘুরে আসতে পারেন এই অসাধারণ লোকেশনগুলো থেকে!

📍 ভিডিওতে দেখা ৬টি গন্তব্য ও লোকেশন লিংকসমূহ

1️⃣ বিডি কায়াকিং (BD Kayaking), দিয়াবাড়ি উত্তরা
📍 অবস্থান: পিলার ১৭৩-এর পাশে, দিয়াবাড়ি লেক, উত্তরা, ঢাকা
🕒 খোলা থাকে: সকাল ৯টা – রাত ৮টা (সপ্তাহের ৭ দিনই)
💰 ভাড়া: ২ সিট কায়াক – ২০০ টাকা (৪০ মিনিট), ৩ সিট কায়াক – ৩০০ টাকা
📍 গুগল ম্যাপ: https://maps.app.goo.gl/2FfYyRYYj2vYQwhdA

2️⃣ বেঙ্গল বই (Bengal Boi), ধানমন্ডি
📍 অবস্থান: ধানমন্ডি ২৭ নম্বর রোডের পাশে, ঢাকা
☕ প্রবেশ একদম ফ্রি | ভিতরে বই পড়া ও কফি কর্নার
📍 গুগল ম্যাপ: https://maps.app.goo.gl/8aHcV6sGJ4TxVEMh8

3️⃣ শেফস টেবিল কোর্টসাইড (Chef’s Table Courtside) ও গো-কার্ট (GoKart), মাদানী এভিনিউ
📍 অবস্থান: ইউনাইটেড সিটি, মাদানী এভিনিউ, ঢাকা
🕒 খোলা থাকে: সকাল ১০টা – রাত ১১টা
💰 গো-কার্ট রাইড: ৬০০ টাকা থেকে (৩ ল্যাপ)
📍 গুগল ম্যাপ: https://maps.app.goo.gl/p3ApLb5LrJcdz8w46

4️⃣ বাংলাদেশ সামরিক জাদুঘর (Bangladesh Military Museum), বিজয় সরণি
📍 অবস্থান: বিজয় সরণি, নভো থিয়েটারের পাশে, ঢাকা
🕒 খোলা থাকে: বৃহস্পতিবার থেকে মঙ্গলবার সকাল ১০:৩০ – সন্ধ্যা ৬:৩০
🚫 বন্ধ থাকে: বুধবার ও সরকারি ছুটির দিন
💰 প্রবেশমূল্য: জনপ্রতি ১৫০ টাকা (মূল্য পরিবর্তন হতে পারে)
📍 গুগল ম্যাপ: https://maps.app.goo.gl/bYbQ4MFe5mF8Y6yT9

5️⃣ নীলা মার্কেট (Neela Market), পূর্বাচল ৩০০ ফিট
📍 অবস্থান: পূর্বাচল এক্সপ্রেসওয়ে, বালু ব্রিজের পাশে
🕒 খোলা থাকে: বিকেল ৩টা থেকে গভীর রাত পর্যন্ত
🍴 বিশেষত্ব: হাঁসের মাংস, চালের রুটি, পিঠা, বারবিকিউ, চা
📍 গুগল ম্যাপ: https://maps.app.goo.gl/C2e3hPBkDzZgBDff9

6️⃣ হারবাইদ ওয়াকওয়ে (Harbaid Walkway), টঙ্গী
📍 অবস্থান: হারবাইদ, তুরাগ নদীর পাড়, টঙ্গী, গাজীপুর
💰 প্রবেশমূল্য: নেই (একদম ফ্রি)
🕒 খোলা থাকে: সারাদিন
📍 গুগল ম্যাপ: https://maps.app.goo.gl/mY3z5LGf8kDKdvKJ7
